While superposition has mostly been studied in idealized settings where features are sparse and uncorrelated, the authors introduce BOWS (Bag-of-Words Superposition) to demonstrate that when features are correlated, interference can be constructive rather than just noise. Features are arranged according to co-activation patterns, giving rise to semantic clusters and cyclical structures observed in real language models.

news.title: Think Before You Lie: How Reasoning Improves Honesty
news.summary: This paper investigates whether reasoning in LLMs increases honesty. Contrary to humans, who become less honest given time to deliberate, the authors find that reasoning consistently increases honesty across scales. The effect is not just about reasoning content - the underlying geometry of representational space contributes to this effect. Deceptive regions within this space are more easily destabilized by input paraphrasing.

news.title: Thinking to Recall: How Reasoning Unlocks Parametric Knowledge in LLMs
news.summary: While reasoning in LLMs plays a natural role in math, code generation, and multi-hop factual questions, its effect on simple, single-hop factual questions remains unclear. This paper finds that enabling reasoning substantially expands the capability boundary of model's parametric knowledge recall, unlocking correct answers that are otherwise effectively unreachable. The authors identify two key driving mechanisms: (1) a computational buffer effect where the model uses generated reasoning tokens to perform latent computation; and (2) factual priming where generating topically related facts acts as a semantic bridge.

news.title: French Aid Worker Among Three Killed in DR Congo Drone Strikes
news.summary: Witnesses say drone strikes hit a residential building frequently occupied by expatriates and aid workers in Goma. Attack raises humanitarian concerns in conflict zone.

news.title: G7 Agrees to Record Emergency Oil Release as Prices Surge
news.summary: Group of seven nations welcomes idea of releasing oil reserves in response to price surge since US-Israel war with Iran began. Move aims to stabilize global energy markets.

news.title: Trump Says US "Not Finished Yet" in Iran as Israel Expands Lebanon Campaign
news.summary: US president might learn that starting wars is much easier than ending them, writes BBC's international editor. Israeli military responds to Hezbollah strikes from Iran, warns Beirut residents to evacuate.

news.title: Ukraine Says It Hit Russian "Missile Component" Plant
news.summary: Russia says attack on a facility in Bryansk hit civilians and could not have been carried out without British help. Incident raises questions about targeting of dual-use technology infrastructure.

news.title: Amazon Expands Health AI Agent Access to Consumers
news.summary: Amazon expands access to its Health AI agent, a HIPAA-compliant tool that can answer general health questions, analyze medical records, and connect users to medical professionals through One Medical. The service is being made more widely available.

news.title: Grammarly Faces Backlash Over "Expert Review" Feature
news.summary: Writing enhancement tool Grammarly's "expert review" feature sparked controversy after its AI agent masqueraded as Verge staff members. Superhuman has now created an opt-out process allowing writers to disable the feature via email.

news.title: Meta Announces MTIA 300 Chip for Consumer AI Workloads
news.summary: Meta launches the MTIA 300 chip designed for training ranking and recommendation systems across Instagram and Facebook. While primarily for AI infrastructure, the chip family (MTIA 400, 450, 500) will support generative AI inference in consumer applications through 2027.

news.title: Microsoft Signals Cross-Platform Future for Xbox at GDC
news.summary: Microsoft's presence at Game Developers Conference suggested emphasis on building next-gen Xbox games for PC, with messaging that "cross-platform is the future of Xbox ecosystem." The company encouraged developers to build for Xbox on PC first.

news.title: The Verge Launches "Continue" Video Game Magazine
news.summary: Editors Mark Serrels and Jackson Ryan launched a new video game publication called Continue via Kickstarter, promising to celebrate and interrogate video game culture, creativity, and community. The campaign has already exceeded its funding goal.

news.title: Anthropic Upgrades Claude's Spreadsheet and Slide Deck Skills
news.summary: Claude can now communicate across Excel and PowerPoint, saving users from switching tabs or re-explaining datasets. Anthropic says Claude "carries conversation across apps without losing track of what's happening in either."

news.title: Grammarly Allows Opt-Out from "Expert Review" After Backlash
news.summary: After widespread criticism of its AI agent masquerading as Verge staff members, Superhuman now says writers can email them to opt out of Grammarly's "expert review" feature.

news.title: Hugging Face Launches Modular Diffusers Framework
news.summary: New composable building blocks for diffusion pipelines enable easier experimentation and customization of generative AI models. Modular approach allows mixing and matching components for specific use cases.

news.title: Sora Could Soon Get More Accessible in ChatGPT
news.summary: OpenAI's video generator Sora may become more accessible within ChatGPT, potentially leading to a new wave of deepfakes and creative video content generation at scale.

news.title: Study Analyzes 16 Open-Source RL Libraries
news.summary: Comprehensive analysis of open-source reinforcement learning libraries reveals best practices and lessons learned for building more efficient RL systems. Insights into token management, training efficiency, and agent architecture.

news.title: ConfCtrl: Enabling Precise Camera Control in Video Diffusion via Confidence-Aware Interpolation
news.summary: Addresses novel view synthesis from two input images under large viewpoint changes. ConfCtrl uses a confidence-weighted projected point cloud latent combined with noise as conditioning, applying a Kalman-inspired predict-update mechanism that balances pose-driven predictions with noisy geometric observations to generate stable, geometry-aware novel views.

news.title: One-Eval: An Agentic System for Automated and Traceable LLM Evaluation
news.summary: One-Eval converts natural-language evaluation requests into executable, traceable evaluation workflows. The system integrates NL2Bench for intent structuring, BenchResolve for benchmark resolution and dataset acquisition, and Metrics & Reporting for task-aware metric selection with human-in-the-loop checkpoints for review and rollback.

news.title: ReCoSplat: Autoregressive Feed-Forward Gaussian Splatting Using Render-and-Compare
news.summary: ReCoSplat is an autoregressive feed-forward Gaussian Splatting model supporting posed or unposed inputs with or without camera intrinsics. The Render-and-Compare (ReCo) module renders current reconstruction from predicted viewpoint and compares it with incoming observation, providing stable conditioning that compensates for pose errors. Includes hybrid KV cache compression reducing cache size by 90%+ for 100+ frames.

news.title: Amazon Launches HIPAA-Compliant Health AI Agent
news.summary: Amazon expands access to its Health AI agent, a HIPAA-compliant tool that can answer general health questions, analyze medical records, and connect users to medical professionals through One Medical.

news.title: Amazon Strengthens AI Coding Guardrails After AWS Outages
news.summary: Following recent AWS outages linked to AI coding agent errors, Amazon now requires senior engineer sign-off for any AI-assisted changes by junior and mid-level engineers.

news.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Sonnet 4.6
news.summary: Claude Sonnet 4.6 delivers frontier performance across coding, agents, and professional work at scale, marking another significant upgrade to the Claude family.

news.title: Anthropic Releases Statement on Department of War Collaboration
news.summary: Dario Amodei releases a statement on Anthropic's discussions with the Department of War regarding national security uses of AI, addressing ethical considerations and public concerns.

news.title: Google Expands Gemini in Chrome Globally
news.summary: Google's built-in Gemini AI assistant in Chrome now supports users in Canada, New Zealand, and India with over 50 languages, enabling screen-aware assistance and cross-tab actions.

news.title: Hugging Face Introduces Storage Buckets
news.summary: Hugging Face launches Storage Buckets on the Hub, providing enhanced storage and management capabilities for AI models and datasets.

news.title: IBM Releases Granite 4.0 1B Speech Model
news.summary: IBM launches Granite 4.0 1B Speech, a compact multilingual speech model specifically built for edge deployment with optimized performance.

news.title: Meta Announces New AI Chip Family MTIA 300
news.summary: Meta launches the MTIA 300 chip designed for training ranking and recommendation systems, with upcoming MTIA 400, 450, and 500 models planned for generative AI inference through 2027.

news.title: NVIDIA and Hugging Face Release Code Concepts Dataset
news.summary: Code Concepts is a large-scale synthetic dataset generated from programming concept seeds, designed to advance AI code generation and understanding capabilities.

news.title: Ulysses Sequence Parallelism for Million-Token Contexts
news.summary: Researchers introduce Ulysses Sequence Parallelism, a distributed training technique that enables training with million-token contexts, pushing the boundaries of long-context AI models.

news.title: Compiling Temporal Numeric Planning into Discrete PDDL+: Extended Version
news.summary: Presents a practical compilation method for temporal numeric planning with durative actions into PDDL+. Provides actual compilation methods and semantic capture, only assuming non-self-overlapping actions. The compilation is polynomial and keeps plan length up to a constant factor.

news.title: Examining Reasoning LLMs-as-Judges in Non-Verifiable LLM Post-Training
news.summary: Investigates whether reasoning judges can benefit from inference-time scaling in non-verifiable LLM post-training. Finds that non-reasoning judges can easily be reward hacked, while reasoning judges trained with RL-based alignment may lead to policy risks that achieve strong performance when evaluated by gold-standard judges.

news.title: Portfolio of Solving Strategies in CEGAR-based Object Packing and Scheduling for Sequential 3D Printing
news.summary: Proposes Portfolio-CEGAR-SEQ algorithm for object arrangement and scheduling in sequential 3D printing. Achieves this by parallelizing existing CEGAR-SEQ algorithm. Experimental evaluation shows Portfolio-CEGAR-SEQ outperforms original CEGAR-SEQ, often using fewer printing plates when scheduling batches for multiple printing plates.

news.title: Qantas Agrees to Pay $74M Over COVID-19 Travel Voucher Refunds
news.summary: The case relates to cancelled flights during the pandemic, for which customers were given credits instead of cash. This settlement provides closure for customers affected by COVID-related disruptions to Qantas services.

news.title: US Military Rescue Efforts Face Challenges in Iraq
news.summary: American military says rescue operations faced difficulties after a refueling plane crashed in Iraq. Meanwhile, Israel continues strikes against Iran and Lebanon. This ongoing regional conflict continues to impact security and infrastructure in the Middle East.

news.title: Young Party Wins Massive Mandate in Nepal Election
news.summary: Rastriya Swatantra Party has won a large majority in a Nepal election dominated by issues including corruption. This political shift may influence policy decisions affecting technology sector and international cooperation.

news.title: Cross-Species Transfer Learning for Electrophysiology-to-Transcriptomics Mapping in Cortical GABAergic Interneurons
news.summary: Replicates and extends the electrophysiology-to-transcriptomics framework using Patch-seq datasets from both mouse and human cortex. Focuses on GABAergic inhibitory interneurons with comparable subclass structure across species. Demonstrates that mouse-to-human transfer learning provides measurable gains for human subclass prediction using attention-based BiLSTM operating on IPFX feature-family representation.

news.title: Factorized Neural Implicit DMD for Parametric Dynamics
news.summary: Proposes a physics-coded neural field parameterization of Koopman operator's spectral decomposition for data-driven, model-free modeling of temporal evolution. Uses factorized flow operator that decouples spatial modes and temporal evolution, exposing underlying eigenvalues, modes, and stability for stable long-term rollouts and spectral analysis.

news.title: Protein Counterfactuals via Diffusion-Guided Latent Optimization
news.summary: Introduces MCCOP (Manifold-Constrained Counterfactual Optimization for Proteins), a framework computing minimal, biologically plausible sequence edits that flip model's prediction to desired target state. Balances validity, proximity, and plausibility objectives using pretrained diffusion model as manifold prior. Successfully evaluated on GFP fluorescence rescue, thermodynamic stability enhancement, and E3 ligase activity recovery tasks.

news.title: Big Tech Backs Anthropic in Fight Against Trump Administration
news.summary: A group representing tech giants called government action against Anthropic a "temper tantrum," supporting the AI company against regulatory pressure. Major tech companies are publicly backing Anthropic against government action.

news.title: Big Tech Backs Anthropic in Fight Against Trump Administration
news.summary: A group representing tech giants called government action against Anthropic a "temper tantrum," supporting the AI company against regulatory pressure.

news.title: Epstein Used AI Modelling Agent to Recruit Girls, Women Tell BBC
news.summary: Investigation reveals that modelling AI agents were used by businesses to recruit girls and arrange US visas to visit Jeffrey Epstein, highlighting potential for AI abuse in recruitment.

news.title: Oil Price Volatility: Sharp Swings Raise Concerns About Economic Stability
news.summary: Oil price moves have made headlines since Iran conflict started, but analysis questions why there have been such sharp swings and potential prolonged economic impact.

news.title: Strait of Hormuz: Six Ships Attacked in 48 Hours as Iran War Continues
news.summary: Reports indicate six ships have been attacked in the Gulf in less than 48 hours, bringing total vessels attacked during conflict to 18 and threatening global oil supply.

news.title: G7 Nations Agree to Record Emergency Oil Release as Iran War Escalates
news.summary: Group of Seven nations released strategic oil reserves to stabilize global energy markets in response to price surge since US-Israel war with Iran began. Air strikes have damaged Iranian oil facilities.

news.title: Google and Tesla Join "Utilize" Coalition for Grid Efficiency
news.summary: Google and Tesla have joined a new initiative called "Utilize" that aims to make power grids more efficient through battery storage and virtual power plant technologies. The coalition seeks to make better use of existing electrons in the grid.

news.title: Meta Announces New MTIA 300 Chip for Energy-Efficient AI Inference
news.summary: Meta launched the MTIA 300 chip designed for training ranking and recommendation systems, with upcoming MTIA 400, 450, and 500 models planned for generative AI inference through 2027. Chips will mainly be used for generative AI inference.

news.title: A Hybrid Knowledge-Grounded Framework for Safety and Traceability in Prescription Verification
news.summary: Introduces PharmGraph-Auditor, a system for safe and evidence-grounded prescription auditing. Features a Hybrid Pharmaceutical Knowledge Base (HPKB) implemented under Virtual Knowledge Graph paradigm, unifying relational and graph components. Proposes KB-grounded Chain of Verification (CoV) reasoning paradigm that transforms LLM from unreliable generator into transparent reasoning engine by decomposing audit tasks into verifiable queries.

news.title: Beyond Illusion of Consensus: From Surface Heuristics to Knowledge-Grounded Evaluation in LLM-as-a-Judge
news.summary: Challenges the assumption that high inter-evaluator agreement indicates reliable LLM evaluation. Introduces "Evaluation Illusion" phenomenon where LLM judges anchor scores on surface heuristics rather than substantive quality. Demonstrates that high-quality outputs paradoxically receive least consistent evaluations. Shows that knowledge-driven rubric generation (MERG) produces more meaningful assessment in codified domains.

news.title: Instruction set for representation of graphs
news.summary: Presents IsalGraph, a method for representing structure of any finite, simple graph as a compact string over a nine-character instruction alphabet. Encoding executed by a small virtual machine comprising sparse graph, circular doubly-linked list of graph-node references, and two traversal pointers. Every string over alphabet decodes to a valid graph with no invalid states reachable. Levenshtein distance between IsalGraph strings correlates strongly with graph edit distance (GED).

news.title: Leech Lattice Vector Quantization for Efficient LLM Compression
news.summary: Introduces LLVQ (Leech Lattice Vector Quantization), leveraging the optimal sphere packing and kissing configurations of Leech lattice at 24 dimensions. Extends existing search algorithm to support indexing without materializing codebook, allow angular search over union of Leech lattice shells, and propose fully-parallelisable dequantization kernel.

news.title: Neural Field Thermal Tomography: A Differentiable Physics Framework for Non-Destructive Evaluation
news.summary: Proposes NeFTY (Neural Field Thermal Tomography), a differentiable physics framework for quantitative 3D reconstruction of material properties from transient surface temperature measurements. Parameterizes 3D diffusivity field as a continuous neural field optimized through rigorous numerical solver. Uses discretize-then-optimize paradigm to mitigate spectral bias and ill-posedness in inverse heat conduction.

news.title: Amazon Tightens AI Coding Guardrails After AWS Outages
news.summary: Amazon's eCommerce SVP announced new oversight around AI coding after recent outages linked to AI coding agent errors. Junior and mid-level engineers now require senior engineer sign-off on AI-assisted changes.

news.title: Anthropic Launches New AI Research Institute
news.summary: Co-founder Jack Clark will lead the new Anthropic Institute. Clark stated he had "no concerns" about research funding for the new initiative focused on AI safety and research.

news.title: Claude Can Now Generate Interactive Visuals Automatically
news.summary: Claude will automatically generate interactive visuals based on your conversation, or you can ask it directly. This new capability enhances data visualization and communication.

news.title: Claude Gains Cross-App Capabilities with Excel and PowerPoint
news.summary: Anthropic upgraded Claude to communicate across Excel and PowerPoint, eliminating the need to switch tabs or re-explain datasets. Claude can now carry conversations across apps without losing track.

news.title: Claude Sonnet 4.6 Released by Anthropic
news.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Sonnet 4.6, delivering frontier performance across coding, agents, and professional workloads at scale. The new model represents a significant upgrade in AI capabilities.

news.title: Google Brings Gemini in Chrome to More Countries
news.summary: Google expanded Gemini in Chrome to Canada, New Zealand, and India, adding support for more than 50 languages including Spanish, French, Hindi, and Chinese.

news.title: Meta Launches New MTIA 300 AI Chip
news.summary: Meta introduced the Meta Training and Inference Accelerator (MTIA) 300 chip, designed to train ranking and recommendations systems across Instagram and Facebook. Upcoming MTIA 400, 450, and 500 will handle generative AI inference.

news.title: OpenAI Acquires Promptfoo AI Security Platform
news.summary: OpenAI acquired Promptfoo, an AI security platform used by 25% of Fortune 500 companies. The tool helps identify and remediate vulnerabilities in AI systems during development.

news.title: Pentagon Ends Talks with Anthropic
news.summary: The Pentagon says "the talks are over" with Anthropic regarding national security uses of AI. Michael stated, "I don't think there's a scenario where this gets resolved in that way," according to Bloomberg.

news.title: Yann LeCun's Startup Raises $1 Billion for AI World Models
news.summary: AI godfather Yann LeCun's Paris-based startup, Advance Machine Intelligence, raised $1 billion to build AI world models. This represents one of the largest AI funding rounds in 2026.

news.title: EnTransformer: Deep Generative Transformer for Multivariate Probabilistic Forecasting
news.summary: Most probabilistic forecasting approaches rely on restrictive parametric likelihoods and struggle with complex joint predictive distributions. EnTransformer integrates regression and stochastic learning paradigms to model conditional distributions without parametric assumptions.

news.title: Fair-PaperRec: Multi-Layer Perceptron for Bias Mitigation and Quality Optimization in Paper Recommendation
news.summary: Despite frequent double-blind review, demographic biases still disadvantage underrepresented groups in post-review paper acceptance decisions. Fair-PaperRec penalizes demographic disparities while preserving quality through intersectional criteria.

news.title: SLIP: Learning Transferable Sensor Models via Language-Informed Pretraining
news.summary: Open-source framework for learning language-aligned representations that generalize across diverse sensor setups. SLIP integrates contrastive alignment with sensor-conditioned captioning, enabling zero-shot transfer without additional training.

news.title: Last update was at 08:00 - no new developments in past 8 hours
news.summary: All major news from the past day (Iran air assault, Middle East deployment, Iran leadership question, Iraq crash) was captured in the 08:00 report. Current time is 16:08, waiting for new significant events.

news.title: Iranians question leadership after Mojtaba Khamenei's first TV address
news.summary: Supreme leader's first public address delivered via presenter on state TV, sparking questions about who's actually in charge following months of uncertainty.

news.title: US launches major air assault on Iran's Kharg Island
news.summary: Trump described devastating attack as "short-term excursion" but warned war could last longer. Reports suggest all 90% of Iran's oil exports pass through Kharg Island, now primary target.

news.title: US Marine reinforcements deployed to Middle East
news.summary: Additional US warships and Marines moving to region as Trump administration escalates military presence. Amphibious ready group from Japan may provide operational support.

news.title: US may move missile defense system to Middle East
news.summary: Reports say US may be relocating parts of THAAD anti-ballistic system to Middle East despite South Korean opposition. Could strengthen regional military alliances.

news.title: US refuelling plane crashes in western Iraq; six crew killed
news.summary: US Central Command confirms all six crew members died after aircraft went down over western Iraq. Investigation ongoing into cause of crash.

news.title: Apple Arcade Adds Dredge+ and Unpacking+ in April
news.summary: Apple's gaming subscription is getting two new indie games on April 2nd: Dredge+, a spooky fishing simulator, and Unpacking+, a cozy interior decorating/puzzle game.

news.title: Meta Postpones 'Avocado' AI Model Until May
news.summary: The company postponed its next AI model, codenamed Avocado, from this month until at least May. Performance apparently falls short of rivals like Google.

news.title: Netflix Acquires Ben Affleck's AI Startup for $600M
news.summary: Netflix purchased Artistan, an AI startup founded by Ben Affleck, for around $600 million. The company reportedly has a different approach to generative AI.

news.title: Nintendo Teases Lego Super Mario Minifigures Coming in 2027
news.summary: Nintendo teased new Lego Super Mario minifigures for 2027, following successful S10 and S10 Lite controllers. The announcement was shared on Mario Day's eve.

news.title: Amazon Expands Alexa Plus with New "Sassy" Personality
news.summary: Amazon added "Sassy" option featuring unfiltered personality with razor-sharp wit, playful sarcasm, and occasional censored profanity. Requires adult-only verification checks.

news.title: Google and Tesla Join "Utilize" Coalition for Grid Efficiency
news.summary: Both companies joined new initiative aiming to make power grids more efficient through battery storage and virtual power plant technologies.

news.title: Meta Launches MTIA 300 AI Chip for Inference
news.summary: New MTIA 300 chip designed to train ranking and recommendations systems across Instagram and Facebook. Upcoming MTIA 400, 450, and 500 will handle all workloads, mainly for generative AI inference.

news.title: A Distributed Serving System for Any-to-Any Multimodal Models
news.summary: Cornserve provides a flexible task abstraction for expressing Any-to-Any model computation graphs, enabling component disaggregation and independent scaling.

news.title: A Robust and Efficient Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning Framework for Traffic Signal Control
news.summary: MARL framework with Turning Ratio Randomization, stability-oriented Exponential Phase Duration Adjustment, and Neighbor-Based Observation scheme validated in VISSIM traffic simulator.

news.title: Benchmarking LLMs on Hard Topological Reasoning
news.summary: TopoBench benchmark shows current models solve fewer than 25% of hard topological grid puzzles, revealing significant spatial reasoning limitations.

news.title: Increasing intelligence in AI agents can worsen collective outcomes
news.summary: When resources are scarce, AI model diversity and reinforcement learning increase dangerous system overload, though tribe formation lessens this risk. Crossover point depends on capacity-to-population ratio.

news.title: On Information Self-Locking in Reinforcement Learning for Active Reasoning of LLM agents
news.summary: RL agents trained with outcome-based rewards often suffer from information self-locking: they cease to ask informative questions and struggle to internalize already-obtained information.

news.title: Amazon Strengthens AI Coding Guardrails After AWS Outages
news.summary: Amazon's eCommerce SVP announced that junior and mid-level engineers will now require senior engineer sign-off for any AI-assisted code changes following recent system outages.

news.title: Google Photos Allows Users to Disable AI Search
news.summary: New toggle lets users choose between classic and AI-powered "Ask Photos" modes when searching through app.

news.title: AI toys misread children's emotions, researchers warn
news.summary: In first study of its kind, Cambridge researchers found AI toys could misread some children's emotions. Concerns raised about child development and AI interpretation accuracy.

news.title: Australian businessman found guilty of working for suspected Chinese spies
news.summary: Csergo argued his reports did not contain any secrets but only information available on internet. Case highlights espionage concerns involving technology and information security.

news.title: Balen Shah's RSP achieves landslide victory in historic election
news.summary: A young party led by a rapper has achieved a feat many thought impossible in Nepal: a sweeping majority. First election since violent youth-led uprising toppled government in 2025.

news.title: Iran's supreme leader delivers first address via state TV
news.summary: Iranians are questioning who's really in charge after Mojtaba Khamenei's first address came via a presenter on state TV, following months of uncertainty.

news.title: Six crew members killed after US refuelling plane crashes in Iraq
news.summary: The US Central Command says all six crew members died after a refuelling aircraft went down over western Iraq. Investigation ongoing.

news.title: Epic and Google Settlement Awaits Judge's Decision
news.summary: Epic and Google are settling their legal battle, but the US version of the plan still rests in Judge Donato's hands. He's asking for "friend of the court" briefs in early April.

news.title: Microsoft: Future of Xbox Ecosystem is PC
news.summary: Microsoft signaled at GDC that "cross-platform is future of Xbox ecosystem." The company's booth featured signs promoting "Build for Xbox on PC" alongside teasing Project Helix hardware.

news.title: Even frontier LLMs struggle with hard topological reasoning
news.summary: New benchmark TopoBench shows current models solve fewer than 25% of hard topological grid puzzles, revealing significant limitations in spatial reasoning capabilities.

news.title: Adaptive Length Video Tokenization for Efficient Visual Autoregressive Generation
news.summary: Introduces EVATok, a framework for efficient video tokenization that dynamically assigns tokens based on video complexity rather than using uniform allocation across temporal blocks. Uses lightweight routers to predict optimal token assignments, achieving substantial efficiency improvements while maintaining quality.

news.title: Benchmarking and Advancing Scientific Multimodal Document Reasoning
news.summary: Introduces SciMDR, a 300K QA pair dataset across 20K scientific papers with explicit reasoning chains, using a synthesize-and-reground framework. Also releases SciMDR-Eval, an expert-annotated benchmark for scientific multimodal comprehension.

news.title: Omni-Motion Controlled Multi-Subject Video Customization with Latent Identity Reinforcement Learning
news.summary: Presents DreamVideo-Omni, a unified framework for harmonious multi-subject video customization with omni-motion control. Uses condition-aware 3D rotary positional embedding, hierarchical motion injection, and latent identity reward feedback learning to preserve identity across complex motions.

news.title: Separable Neural Architectures as a Primitive for Unified Predictive and Generative Intelligence
news.summary: Formalizes Separable Neural Architecture (SNA), unifying additive, quadratic, and tensor-decomposed neural models. Reveals structural analogy between chaotic spatiotemporal dynamics and linguistic autoregression by treating continuous states as smooth, separable embeddings.

news.title: Temporal Straightening for Latent Planning
news.summary: Introduces temporal straightening for representation learning in latent planning, using curvature regularizers to encourage locally straightened latent trajectories. Reduces curvature to make Euclidean distance a better proxy for geodesic distance.

news.title: Amazon adds "Sassy" personality to Alexa Plus
news.summary: Amazon expanded Alexa Plus personality styles with a new "Sassy" option, described as "unfiltered personality with razor-sharp wit, playful sarcasm, and occasional censored profanity." The feature requires additional age verification checks.

news.title: Amazon expands access to Health AI agent
news.summary: Amazon launched a HIPAA-compliant Health AI tool similar to ChatGPT for Healthcare, designed to answer general health questions, analyze medical records, and connect users to medical professionals through One Medical.

news.title: Anthropic upgrades Claude's spreadsheet and slide deck skills
news.summary: Claude can now communicate across Excel and PowerPoint, saving users from switching tabs or re-explaining datasets. Anthropic describes this as Claude "carrying the conversation across apps without losing track of what's happening in either."

news.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite: Built for intelligence at scale
news.summary: Google DeepMind releases Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite, a new model designed for scalable intelligence deployment.

news.title: Google brings Gemini in Chrome to more countries
news.summary: Users in Canada, New Zealand, and India can now access Chrome's built-in Gemini AI assistant. The feature has added support for more than 50 languages and can help with tasks like sending messages in Gmail and creating product comparison tables.

news.title: Hugging Face introduces Storage Buckets on the Hub
news.summary: Hugging Face announced Storage Buckets, a new feature on the Hugging Face Hub that provides enhanced storage capabilities for AI model and dataset artifacts.

news.title: Meta delays 'Avocado' AI model until at least May
news.summary: Meta postponed its next AI model, codenamed Avocado, from this month until at least May. Performance reportedly falls short of rivals like Google. The company has spent billions trying to catch up.

news.title: Pentagon says "talks are over" with Anthropic
news.summary: The Department of Defense indicates that negotiations with Anthropic regarding AI partnership have concluded without resolution, following earlier statements from Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei on national security uses of AI.

news.title: Project Genie: Experimenting with infinite, interactive worlds
news.summary: Google DeepMind introduces Project Genie, an experimental platform exploring infinite, interactive virtual worlds built with AI technology.

news.title: Yann LeCun's Paris startup Advance Machine Intelligence raises $1B
news.summary: The AI startup founded by AI godfather Yann LeCun in Paris has raised $1 billion to build AI world models, marking a significant investment in next-generation AI infrastructure.

news.title: Iran War Escalates: US Rescue Plane Crashes, Israel Strikes Tehran
news.summary: BBC reports US Central Command saying rescue operations face difficulties after a refueling aircraft went down over western Iraq. Israel launches a new wave of strikes against Tehran, with footage showing an explosion near a pro-establishment rally.

news.title: Nepal Election: Young Rapper's Party Sweeps to Huge Mandate
news.summary: Balen Shah's Rastriya Swatantra Party won a large majority in Nepal's election. This is considered a political earthquake in a country dominated by issues including corruption.

news.title: No New Global Events in Last 8 Hours
news.summary: Sources checked (Reuters World, BBC World, ABC News Australia) show no new global developments with tech/economic impact in the last 8 hours. Latest global news remains from March 13-15, with major stories including Iran war updates, Middle East tensions, and US diplomatic efforts.

news.title: Education & EdTech Sector Quiet Over Weekend
news.summary: Sources checked (EdSurge, TechCrunch Education) show no new Education & EdTech developments in the last 24 hours. Latest news remains from March 13-14 (Friday-Saturday).

news.title: Are we outsourcing our souls to artificial intelligence?
news.summary: ABC reports growing trend of people turning to AI companions during personal crises, treating chatbots as confidantes and even ways to speak to deceased loved ones. Digital Barbara, an AI reconstruction of a deceased woman, demonstrates emerging "grief bot" industry where users can converse with recreated personalities of dead relatives for US$10-14 per 100 messages.

news.title: Hamas calls on Iran to halt attacks on Gulf states
news.summary: Hamas issued rare public appeal urging its key ally Iran to stop attacking neighbouring countries, calling on regional nations to "preserve bonds of brotherhood" amid ongoing conflict. Meanwhile, Iran's retaliatory drone strikes have hit civilian infrastructure in UAE, Kuwait, and other Gulf states, killing at least 18 people.

news.title: Trump urges international naval deployment to Strait of Hormuz
news.summary: US President Donald Trump has renewed calls for China, France, Japan, South Korea, UK and others to send warships to Strait of Hormuz amid escalating Iran tensions. Trump wrote on Truth Social that he hoped other nations would help secure the oil shipping lane, while Iran threatened to retaliate against regional infrastructure hosting US interests.

news.title: No New Major Education & EdTech Announcements Today
news.summary: Sources checked (TechCrunch, Bloomberg FT, 36Kr, EdSurge, HolonIQ) show no new major Education & EdTech news in the last 24 hours. Most recent EdTech developments are from March 13-14.

news.title: Adaptive Length Video Tokenization for Efficient Visual Autoregressive Generation
news.summary: EVATok framework estimates optimal token assignments for each video to balance reconstruction quality against computational cost. Uses lightweight routers to predict optimal assignments and trains adaptive tokenizers that encode videos based on predicted assignments.

news.title: Benchmarking and Advancing Scientific Multimodal Document Reasoning
news.summary: Introduces SciMDR, a 300K QA dataset with explicit reasoning chains across 20K scientific papers. Uses a two-stage synthesize-and-reground framework to balance scale, faithfulness, and realism in multimodal document understanding.

news.title: Examining Reasoning LLMs-as-Judges in Non-Verifiable LLM Post-Training
news.summary: This study investigates reasoning LLMs-as-judges in reinforcement learning-based LLM alignment. Research finds that non-reasoning judges lead to reward hacking easily, while reasoning judges can train policies that achieve strong performance by generating adversarial outputs that deceive other LLM-judges.

news.title: Separable Neural Architectures as a Primitive for Unified Predictive and Generative Intelligence
news.summary: Separable Neural Architecture (SNA) formalizes a representational class unifying additive, quadratic, and tensor-decomposed neural models. By constraining interaction order and tensor rank, SNAs factorize high-dimensional mappings into low-arity components.

news.title: The Latent Color Subspace: Emergent Order in High-Dimensional Chaos
news.summary: Develops interpretation of color representation in FLUX.1's VAE latent space, revealing a structure reflecting Hue, Saturation, and Lightness. Introduces fully training-free method in FLUX based solely on closed-form latent-space manipulation.

news.title: Amazon adds "Sassy" personality style to Alexa Plus
news.summary: Amazon expanded Alexa Plus personality styles with a new "Sassy" option featuring an "unfiltered personality with razor-sharp wit, playful sarcasm, and occasional censored profanity." The adult-only feature requires additional verification checks but keeps profanity mild and censored.

news.title: Amazon adds guardrails around AI coding after AWS outages
news.summary: Following recent AWS outages linked to AI coding agent errors, Amazon's eCommerce SVP called an all-hands meeting and announced that junior and mid-level engineers will now require more senior engineers to sign off on any AI-assisted changes. The company is implementing more oversight around AI coding.

news.title: Amazon expands access to Health AI agent
news.summary: Similar to ChatGPT for Healthcare, Amazon's Health AI is a HIPAA-compliant tool designed to answer general health questions, analyze medical records, and connect users to medical professionals through One Medical. The company is expanding access to this service.

news.title: Anthropic upgrades Claude's cross-app capabilities
news.summary: Claude can now communicate across Excel and PowerPoint without losing track of context, saving users from needing to switch tabs or re-explain datasets. Anthropic described this as Claude "carrying the conversation across apps without losing track of what's happening in either."

news.title: Google expands Gemini in Chrome to more countries
news.summary: Google is bringing Chrome's built-in Gemini AI assistant to Canada, New Zealand, and India with support for over 50 languages including Spanish, French, Hindi, and Chinese. The assistant can answer questions about on-screen content, help send messages in Gmail, create comparison tables, and remix images.

news.title: Hugging Face introduces Ulysses Sequence Parallelism
news.summary: Hugging Face announced Ulysses Sequence Parallelism, a new technique for training models with million-token contexts. The method enables training with extremely long context windows, advancing large language model capabilities.

news.title: Hugging Face launches Storage Buckets on the Hub
news.summary: Hugging Face introduced Storage Buckets, a new feature for the Hugging Face Hub that enables more flexible storage management for ML models, datasets, and other assets. This update enhances the platform's infrastructure capabilities.

news.title: Meta delays Avocado AI model due to performance issues
news.summary: Meta postponed its next AI model, codenamed Avocado, from March until at least May due to performance falling short of rivals like Google. The company has spent billions trying to catch up and this would be its first major release since hiring Scale AI CEO Alexandr Wang to revamp its efforts.

news.title: The Pentagon says "talks are over" with Anthropic
news.summary: Anthropic released multiple statements in February and early March 2026 regarding their discussions with the U.S. Department of War/Pentagon about national security uses of AI. The latest statement on March 5, 2026, from CEO Dario Amodei addresses "where things stand" in these discussions.

news.title: Thinking Machines Lab partners with Nvidia on gigawatt-scale AI
news.summary: The AI startup founded by ex-OpenAI executive Mira Murati is teaming up with Nvidia on a "long-term gigawatt scale strategic partnership" to power TML's AI model training. This follows multiple founding members of the startup leaving for OpenAI earlier this year.

news.title: Benchmarking Scientific Multimodal Document Reasoning
news.summary: Introduces SciMDR, a 300K QA dataset for training scientific multimodal comprehension, and SciMDR-Eval for evaluation. The synthesize-and-reground framework balances scale, faithfulness, and realism in scientific document reasoning tasks.

news.title: Defending Hybrid Large Language Models Against Hidden State Poisoning Attacks
news.summary: CLASP model defends against HiSPAs (Hidden State Poisoning Attacks) on SSM-based LLMs using XGBoost classification on block output embeddings. Achieves 95.9% token-level F1 on 2,483 résumés with malicious injections.

news.title: Separable neural architectures as a primitive for unified predictive and generative intelligence
news.summary: SNAs formalize additive, quadratic, and tensor-decomposed neural models with constrained interaction order and tensor rank. Enables distributional modelling of chaotic systems while avoiding nonphysical drift.

news.title: Sparking Scientific Creativity via LLM-Driven Interdisciplinary Inspiration
news.summary: Idea-Catalyst framework systematically identifies interdisciplinary insights to support creative reasoning. Decomposes research goals into domain-specific challenges, retrieves analogous solutions from external disciplines, and ranks them by potential impact.

news.title: The Latent Color Subspace: Emergent Order in High-Dimensional Chaos
news.summary: Discovers a structured representation of color (Hue, Saturation, Lightness) in FLUX.1's VAE latent space. Enables training-free color control through closed-form latent space manipulation.

news.title: "One Battle After Another" Wins Best Picture at Oscars
news.summary: The animated film about a woman and her imaginary friend wins top honor, marking success for animated features at Academy Awards. This reflects growing recognition and success of animated storytelling in cinema.

news.title: Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen Steps Down After 18 Years
news.summary: Adobe CEO is stepping down after 18 years, stating "the next era of creativity is being written right now — shaped by AI, by new workflows and by entirely new forms of expression."

news.title: Moltbook Updates Its Rules About What You and Your AI Can Do
news.summary: Just days after Meta acquired "social" network for AI agents, platform has updated its terms of service, stating that users must be 13 years of age or older to use site and are "solely responsible" for actions taken by their AI agents, whether they "act autonomously or otherwise, and irrespective of whether such actions or omissions were intended."

news.title: Nvidia Announces NemoClaw, a More Secure Version of OpenClaw
news.summary: Nvidia launched NemoClaw, an agentic AI platform that adds privacy and security protections to autonomous AI platform OpenClaw. NemoClaw uses Nvidia Agent Toolkit software to optimize OpenClaw in a single command, running it in an "isolated sandbox" environment that enforces policy-based security, network and privacy guardrails.

news.title: Nvidia Partners on Vera Rubin Space 1 to Launch AI Data Centers in Space
news.summary: Nvidia is working with partners on a new computer called Vera Rubin Space 1 that's going to go out to space and start data centers out in space. This represents a bold move to expand AI infrastructure beyond Earth's limitations.

news.title: Yahoo Escapes Its Verizon Death Spiral and Becomes Profitable Again
news.summary: How Yahoo escaped its Verizon death spiral and became profitable again, marking a major turnaround for tech company. This demonstrates successful business recovery strategy after years of decline under Verizon ownership.

news.title: AI Tool Predicts Over 1,000 Diseases Using Clinical Records and Health Data
news.summary: Computer models are showing potential to draw upon a person's clinical records and current health data to forecast diseases and treatment outcomes. Nature questions if this could presage a new era of preventive medicine.

news.title: Beyond Monogenic Disorders: Clinical Practice Lags Behind Rapid Advances
news.summary: Understanding of genetic mechanisms underlying rare diseases has advanced rapidly over past decade, yet clinical practice still has strong monogenic focus, leaving many individuals undiagnosed. Questions: How should technological advances like long-read sequencing be adopted to increase multivariant testing in the clinic?

news.title: CytomX Surges on Positive Data for 'Masked' ADC in Colorectal Cancer
news.summary: CytomX shares skyrocketed nearly 50% on results that handily beat Wall Street expectations for its 'masked' antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) targeting folate receptor alpha in colorectal cancer.

news.title: Microalgae as Cellular Factories for Molecular Crystals
news.summary: Research shows dinoflagellates accumulate and crystallize nitrogen-rich molecules from aqueous solutions and use these crystals for growth. This behavior might have implications for understanding marine nitrogen cycling. Crystallization behavior can be manipulated to biosynthesize difficult-to-crystallize reflective materials with tailored morphologies and optical properties.

news.title: Structure's GLP-1 Pill Shows 'Best-in-Class' Potential in Obesity Trial
news.summary: Testing revealed weight loss effects some Wall Street analysts believe compare favorably to Lilly's orforglipron, which regulators could approve within weeks. Structure's aleniglipron could disrupt highly competitive obesity drug market.

news.title: The Persistent Barrier to Biotechnology in the Global South
news.summary: The persistent challenge of importing biological research materials to Global South at reasonable cost, within acceptable timeframes, and with manageable regulatory complexity stalls progress before research and innovation can even begin.

news.title: Nvidia Launches NemoClaw, a More Secure Version of OpenClaw
news.summary: Nvidia announced NemoClaw, an agentic AI platform that adds privacy and security protections to the autonomous AI platform OpenClaw. NemoClaw uses Nvidia Agent Toolkit software to optimize OpenClaw in a single command, running it in an "isolated sandbox" environment that enforces policy-based security, network and privacy guardrails.

news.title: CRYSTAL Benchmark for Transparent Multimodal Reasoning Evaluation
news.summary: Introduces CRYSTAL, a diagnostic benchmark with 6,372 instances that evaluates multimodal reasoning through verifiable intermediate steps. Proposes Match F1 (step-level precision/recall via semantic similarity) and Ordered Match F1 (penalizes disordered reasoning chains).

news.title: Efficient LLM Agent Tool Planning via Dual-Feedback Monte Carlo Tree Search and Bidirectional Pruning
news.summary: Presents ToolTree, a novel Monte Carlo tree search-inspired planning paradigm for tool planning. Uses dual-stage LLM evaluation and bidirectional pruning mechanism enabling agents to make informed, adaptive decisions over extended tool-use sequences while pruning less promising branches.

news.title: Learnability and Privacy Vulnerability are Entangled in a Few Critical Weights
news.summary: Observes privacy vulnerability exists in very small fraction of weights, but most of those also critically impact utility performance. Instead of discarding neurons, the proposed mechanism "rewinds" only critical weights for fine-tuning.

news.title: Neuron-Aware Data Selection in Instruction Tuning For Large Language Models
news.summary: NAIT evaluates impact of instruction tuning data on LLM performance by analyzing similarity of neuron activation patterns between IT dataset and target domain capabilities. Constructs reusable neuron activation features from in-domain datasets and selects optimal samples based on similarity to expected activation features.

news.title: Semantic Invariance in Agentic AI
news.summary: Metamorphic testing framework systematically assesses LLM reasoning agent robustness across eight semantic-preserving transformations. Evaluates seven foundation models spanning four architectural families including Hermes, Qwen3, DeepSeek-R1, and GPT-OSS.

news.title: Grammarly AI Agent Impersonation Exposed: Writers Can Opt Out
news.summary: Bluesky user coined term "sloppelganger" for Grammarly's AI agent masquerading as Verge staff members. Superhuman now says writers can email to opt out of "expert review" feature.

news.title: Nvidia Becomes First $5 Trillion Company as AI Dominates Markets
news.summary: The world's first $5 trillion company (currently 4.47 trillion) is strutting its stuff in San Jose for GTC 2026. Expecting major AI and robot announcements, with potential N1 consumer laptop chip reveals.

news.title: Yahoo Escapes Verizon Death Spiral and Becomes Profitable Again
news.summary: How Yahoo escaped its Verizon death spiral and became profitable again, marking a major turnaround for the tech company after years of decline.

news.title: Backlash to Growing AI Use Sparks 'AI-Free' Logo Trend
news.summary: The backlash to growing AI adoption has led to an explosion in attempts to create 'AI-Free' logos and branding that companies could use globally, highlighting public resistance to AI automation.

news.title: India Hopeful Iran Talks Will Ease Hormuz Route for Its Ships
news.summary: Indian Foreign Minister tells FT there was no 'blanket arrangement' for Indian ships to pass through the strategic strait, as Iran war continues to threaten commercial shipping.

news.title: Meta and TikTok Let Harmful Content Rise to Boost Engagement
news.summary: Whistleblowers allege both companies allowed more harmful content on user's feeds, knowing their algorithms ran on outrage to increase engagement metrics.

news.title: Trump Says 'Not Happy' With UK Over Iran War, Warns NATO on Hormuz
news.summary: US President repeats call for allies to assist in securing Strait of Hormuz, saying NATO faces 'very bad future' if countries don't help in Iran conflict. UK's Starmer says Britain won't be drawn into wider war.

news.title: Trump White House Chief Susie Wiles Diagnosed with Breast Cancer
news.summary: Chief of Staff will continue to work 'virtually full time at White House' while undergoing treatment, according to President Trump who announced the diagnosis.

news.title: GSK's RSV Vaccine Wins Broader FDA Clearance for Younger Adults
news.summary: The approval represents a win in what's been a difficult regulatory environment of late for vaccine makers, and could boost uptake of a shot that's posted flattening sales after an initially strong launch.

news.title: Lilly Warns GLP-1 Knockoffs May Be 'Dangerous,' Escalating War with Compounders
news.summary: Lilly issued safety warnings about compounded versions of its obesity drugs, escalating conflicts with compounders who sell cheaper, unregulated alternatives that may contain harmful impurities.

news.title: Servier to Build Cancer Drug Pipeline with $2.5B Day One Acquisition
news.summary: The proposed acquisition would hand Servier a couple experimental drugs in human testing as well as Ojemda, an approved therapy for certain hard-to-treat brain tumors that affect children. This is the year's second-largest biotech acquisition.

news.title: Vima Raises $100M to Pursue Oral Drug for Movement Disorders
news.summary: Incubated by Atlas Venture, biotech startup Vima is advancing a daily pill it sees potentially helping people with dystonia or Parkinson's disease regain control of movement, addressing conditions with significant unmet medical needs.

news.title: Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen Stepping Down After 18 Years
news.summary: Adobe CEO is stepping down after 18 years, stating "the next era of creativity is being written right now — shaped by AI, by new workflows and by entirely new forms of expression."

news.title: Buzzfeed Posts $57.3M Loss After Embracing AI, CEO Plans New AI Apps
news.summary: Three years after Buzzfeed embraced AI using it to generate articles and quiz responses, the company posted a loss of $57.3 million in 2025 and stock dropped to just $0.70. CEO Jonah Peretti still plans to bring "new AI apps" to market.

news.title: Lawsuit Accuses OpenAI of Outputting Near-Identical Copies of Britannica Content
news.summary: A new lawsuit alleges OpenAI's models output near-identical copies of Britannica and Merriam-Webster's content without proper attribution or licensing.

news.title: Moltbook Updates AI Agent Terms: Users Must Be 13+ and "Solely Responsible"
news.summary: Platform updated terms of service requiring users to be 13 years or older and are "solely responsible" for actions taken by their AI agents, whether acting autonomously or otherwise.

news.title: Palantir's Maven Smart System Raises Ethical Concerns
news.summary: Palantir demonstrated Maven Smart System at AIPCon, an AI-powered Kanban board for military targeting. The system allows "anyone or anything to be targeted for a military strike with a 'Left click, right click, left click'" workflow.

news.title: Benchmarking Long-Context ESG Reports for Hallucination Mitigation
news.summary: ESG-Bench is a benchmark dataset for ESG report understanding and hallucination mitigation in LLMs, containing human-annotated QA pairs grounded in real-world ESG report contexts. The paper designs task-specific Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting strategies and fine-tunes multiple SOTA LLMs using CoT-annotated rationales.

news.title: Physically-Plausible Humanoid Motion with Preference Optimization
news.summary: PhysMoDPO uses Direct Preference Optimization to make text-conditioned human motion generation more physically plausible. Unlike prior work that relies on hand-crafted physics-aware heuristics, this framework integrates Whole-Body Controller into training and optimizes diffusion models so outputs are compliant with both physics and original text instructions.

news.title: Amazon Alexa Plus Adds "Sassy" Personality
news.summary: Amazon expanded Alexa Plus personality styles with a new "Sassy" option featuring "unfiltered personality with razor-sharp wit, playful sarcasm, and occasional censored profanity." It's adults-only and requires additional verification.

news.title: Amazon Expands Health AI Agent Access
news.summary: Amazon's Health AI is a HIPAA-compliant tool to answer general health questions, analyze medical records, and connect users to medical professionals through One Medical, similar to ChatGPT for Healthcare.

news.title: Anthropic Upgrades Claude's Spreadsheet and Slide Skills
news.summary: Claude can now communicate across Excel and PowerPoint, saving users from needing to switch tabs or re-explain datasets at every step. Anthropic says Claude "carries the conversation across apps without losing track."

news.title: Google Expands Chrome Gemini to More Countries
news.summary: Users in Canada, New Zealand, and India can now access Chrome's built-in Gemini AI assistant, with support for more than 50 languages including Spanish, French, Hindi, and Chinese. The assistant can help answer questions about screen content and perform actions across Gmail and tabs.

news.title: IBM Granite 4.0 1B Speech Released
news.summary: IBM released Granite 4.0 1B Speech, a compact, multilingual speech model built for edge devices. The model aims to bring high-quality speech recognition and generation to resource-constrained environments.

news.title: LeRobot v0.5.0 Released with Scale Improvements
news.summary: Hugging Face released LeRobot v0.5.0, "Scaling Every Dimension," bringing significant improvements to robotics AI capabilities across multiple dimensions of the platform.

news.title: Meta Delays Avocado AI Model
news.summary: Meta postponed its next AI model, codenamed Avocado, from this month until at least May due to performance falling short of rivals like Google. The company has spent billions trying to catch up.

news.title: Meta Launches MTIA 300 AI Chip
news.summary: The newly-launched Meta Training and Inference Accelerator (MTIA) 300 chip is designed to train ranking and recommendations systems across Instagram and Facebook. Upcoming MTIA 400, 450, and 500 chips will handle generative AI inference through 2027.

news.title: Palantir's Maven Smart System Demo Controversy
news.summary: Palantir demonstrated Maven Smart System at AIPCon, an AI-powered Kanban board for military targeting. The system allows "anyone or anything to be targeted for a military strike with a 'Left click, right click, left click'" workflow, raising ethical concerns.

news.title: Ulysses Sequence Parallelism Enables Million-Token Context Training
news.summary: Hugging Face introduced Ulysses Sequence Parallelism, enabling training with million-token contexts. The technique significantly expands the context window capabilities for large language models.

news.title: Conservative Continuous-Time Treatment Optimization
news.summary: Develops a conservative continuous-time stochastic control framework for treatment optimization from irregularly sampled patient trajectories. The unknown patient dynamics are modeled as a controlled stochastic differential equation with treatment as a continuous-time control. To limit extrapolation, a consistent signature-based MMD regularizer on path space penalizes treatment plans whose induced trajectory distribution deviates from observed trajectories.

news.title: Critical Digital Literacy and Resilience Against Misinformation
news.summary: Misinformation on social media undermines informed decision-making and public trust. Prebunking offers a proactive complement by helping users recognize manipulation tactics before they encounter them in the wild. We present CritiSense, a mobile media-literacy app that builds these skills through short, interactive challenges with instant feedback. It is first multilingual (supporting nine languages) and modular platform, designed for rapid updates across topics and domains.

news.title: SocialOmni: Audio-Visual Social Interaction in Omni Models
news.summary: Omni-modal large language models (LLMs) redefine human-machine interaction by natively integrating audio, vision, and text. However, existing OLM benchmarks remain anchored to static, accuracy-centric tasks, leaving a critical gap in assessing social interactivity, fundamental capacity to navigate dynamic cues in natural dialogues.

news.title: PM Albanese Refuses to Answer Questions on Israel-Palestine War
news.summary: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese declined to answer whether Australia should recognize Palestinian statehood or re-evaluate aid to Israel, maintaining stance as major decisions loom.

news.title: RBA Raises Interest Rates as War Inflation Persists
news.summary: Reserve Bank lifted interest rates for second time in 2026, citing inflation pressures from Iran war impact. The move comes as economists warn of persistent inflation despite cooling in other sectors.

news.title: Treasurer Chalmers Raises Concerns Over Labor Budget Cuts
news.summary: Treasurer Jim Chalmers expressed concerns over Labor government's budget cuts, warning that spending reductions could impact essential services and economic programs across multiple sectors.

news.title: Google Expands Personal Intelligence Access to Free Tier
news.summary: Free-tier users now have access to Personal Intelligence in the Gemini app and Gemini in Chrome.

news.title: Jensen Huang Defends DLSS 5: "Gamers Are Completely Wrong"
news.summary: Nvidia CEO critical reaction to backlash over anti-cheating tech, saying gamers "don't understand what they're talking about."

news.title: Nemotron 3 Nano 4B: Compact Hybrid Model for Efficient Local AI
news.summary: Hugging Face released Nemotron 3 Nano 4B, a compact hybrid model combining efficiency and performance for local AI deployment.

news.title: Are Dilemmas and Conflicts in LLM Alignment Solvable? A View from Priority Graph
news.summary: Taxonomizes diverse conflicts and dilemmas in LLM behavior using priority graphs. Shows unified stable LLM alignment is challenging because graphs are neither static nor consistent across contexts. Also reveals vulnerability to "priority hacking" for adversarial manipulation of context.

news.title: Talk, Evaluate, Diagnose: User-aware Agent Evaluation with Automated Error Analysis
news.summary: TED framework for evaluating user-aware agents: (1) Talk leverages reusable generic templates, (2) Evaluate adapts existing datasets with subgoals and natural language grading, (3) Diagnose introduces automated error analysis tool to uncover inconsistencies. Accepted at ICLR 2026.

news.title: Understanding Reasoning in LLMs through Strategic Information Allocation under Uncertainty
news.summary: Decomposes reasoning into procedural information and epistemic verbalization to handle uncertainty. Shows strong reasoning performance is driven by uncertainty externalization rather than specific surface tokens. Empirical results demonstrate importance of epistemic verbalization for continued information acquisition.

news.title: Hugging Face Introduces Storage Buckets
news.summary: Hugging Face launched Storage Buckets, a new feature that enhances the Hub's storage capabilities for large datasets and model artifacts. This addresses scalability needs for growing AI models and datasets.

news.title: Reserve Bank Raises Interest Rates as War Inflation Persists
news.summary: RBA lifted interest rates for second time in 2026, citing inflation pressures from Iran war impact. The move comes as economists warn of persistent inflation despite cooling in other sectors.

news.title: Students Rethinking Degrees as AI Reshapes White-Collar Jobs
news.summary: As AI automation expands across knowledge work, students increasingly question the relevance of traditional university degrees. Experts say no occupation is completely immune from AI, but the rapid transformation of professional sectors is forcing young people to reconsider career pathways.

news.title: Trump Lashes Out at NATO Allies Over Iran War Support
news.summary: US President Trump criticized NATO allies including Australia for supporting Iran war, saying America "no longer needs" their help. Comments come as European nations decline US requests to protect the Strait of Hormuz.

news.title: US Attorney General Bondi Summons Congress in Epstein Case
news.summary: Bondi formally summoned Congress amid renewed investigations into connections between powerful figures and Jeffrey Epstein. The case continues to have significant political and legal implications.

news.title: Democratizing Frontier Search Agents by Fully Open-Sourcing Training Data
news.summary: Introduces OpenSeeker, the first fully open-source search agent (model and data) that achieves frontier-level performance. Uses fact-grounded scalable controllable QA synthesis and denoised trajectory synthesis techniques. Trained on only 11.7k synthesized samples via single SFT run.

news.title: Effective Distillation to Hybrid xLSTM Architectures
news.summary: Proposes effective distillation pipeline for xLSTM-based students with an additional merging stage where individually linearized experts are combined into single model. Distills base and instruction-tuned models from Llama, Qwen, and Olmo families.

news.title: How Ranking Beats Structure for Conversational Memory Retrieval
news.summary: SmartSearch retrieves from raw, unstructured conversation history using a fully deterministic pipeline: NER-weighted substring matching for recall, rule-based entity discovery for multi-hop expansion, and CrossEncoder+ColBERT rank fusion.

news.title: Mixture-of-Depths Attention (MoDA)
news.summary: A new attention mechanism allowing each attention head to attend to sequence KV pairs at current layer and depth KV pairs from preceding layers. Addresses signal degradation in deep LLMs where informative features from shallow layers get diluted.

news.title: Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen Stepping Down After 18 Years
news.summary: Adobe's CEO of 18 years is stepping down as the company enters what Narayen calls "the next era of creativity" being shaped by AI.

news.title: Amazon Alexa Plus Adds "Sassy" Personality Style
news.summary: Amazon expanded Alexa Plus with a new "Sassy" option offering an unfiltered personality with playful sarcasm and occasional censored profanity. The feature requires adult verification.

news.title: Anthropic Statements on Department of War Partnership
news.summary: Anthropic released statements from CEO Dario Amodei regarding discussions with the Department of War on national security uses of AI, responding to comments from Secretary of War Pete Hegseth.

news.title: Holotron-12B Released - High Throughput Computer Use Agent
news.summary: Hugging Face announced Holotron-12B, a 12B parameter model designed as a high-throughput computer use agent for autonomous tasks.

news.title: IBM Releases Granite 4.0 1B Speech Model
news.summary: IBM announced Granite 4.0 1B Speech, a compact, multilingual speech model designed for edge deployment with minimal resource requirements.

news.title: Meta Launches MTIA 300 AI Chip
news.summary: Meta announced the new Meta Training and Inference Accelerator (MTIA) 300 chip designed for training ranking and recommendation systems across Instagram and Facebook. Future MTIA 400, 450, and 500 chips will handle generative AI inference.

news.title: Meta's Avocado AI Model Delayed
news.summary: Meta postponed its next AI model, codenamed Avocado, from this month until at least May. Performance reportedly falls short of rivals like Google, despite spending billions to catch up.

news.title: No Major Breaking News Since 04:01 Update
news.summary: Since the 04:01 AM report, no new major AI industry developments have been detected across monitored sources. Previous stories remain current: NemoClaw security platform, Holotron-12B release, OpenAI strategic shift, Nvidia space data centers, Amazon Alexa Sassy personality, Meta Avocado delay, and various product updates from major AI labs.

news.title: Nvidia Announces NemoClaw - Secure OpenClaw Platform
news.summary: Nvidia launched NemoClaw, a more secure version of OpenClaw that runs in an isolated sandbox environment. The platform adds privacy and security protections to autonomous AI agents using Nvidia Agent Toolkit software.

news.title: Nvidia Plans AI Data Centers in Space
news.summary: Nvidia announced partnerships to deploy AI data centers in space with the "Vera Ruben Space 1" computer. The company is solving cooling challenges for space deployment where only radiation exists.

news.title: OpenAI Cuts Back on "Side Quests" Projects
news.summary: OpenAI CEO of applications Fidji Simo told staff the company will prioritize coding and enterprise users, cutting projects including Sora deepfake machine, Atlas browser, and AI-powered gadgets.

news.title: Amodei Issues Statement on US National Security AI Usage
news.summary: AI company Amodei clarified its AI systems' usage with the U.S. Department of Defense, explaining the national security context and defensive applications of its technology.

news.title: Anthropic Explains AI Safety and Ad Incentives Mismatch
news.summary: Anthropic published an analysis explaining why advertising-driven AI assistant models can misalign incentives for creating genuinely helpful AI assistants. The company highlights the importance of designing systems that optimize for user benefit rather than engagement metrics.

news.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Sonnet 4.6
news.summary: Anthropic released a new Claude Sonnet 4.6 model with improved performance for coding, agent workflows, and professional tasks. The update focuses on enhanced capabilities for developers and enterprises.

news.title: Evaluating the Quality and Efficiency of Pre-Training on Downstream Task Performance
news.summary: This paper evaluates the quality and efficiency of using pre-training on downstream task performance rather than training from scratch for large vision-language models. The authors show this approach can achieve comparable or better performance with significantly less compute.

news.title: Learnability and Privacy Vulnerability in Critical Weights
news.summary: Observes privacy vulnerability exists in a very small fraction of weights that also critically impact utility performance. The proposed mechanism "rewinds" only critical weights for fine-tuning, exhibiting outperforming resilience against membership inference attacks.

news.title: NAIT: Neuron-Aware Data Selection in Instruction Tuning
news.summary: NAIT evaluates how instruction tuning data affects model performance by analyzing neuron activation pattern similarity. Training on just 10% of Alpaca-GPT4 data subset selected by NAIT consistently outperforms methods relying on external advanced models.

news.title: On the Power of Iterative Refinement in Training Foundation Models: A Data-Centric Approach
news.summary: Proposes a data-centric approach to iteratively refine foundation models without full retraining from scratch. The method uses a curated dataset to update model weights incrementally.

news.title: Semantic Invariance in Agentic AI
news.summary: A metamorphic testing framework systematically assesses LLM reasoning agent robustness across eight semantic-preserving transformations. Finds that smaller models like Qwen3-30B-A3B can achieve higher invariance (79.6%) than larger models.

news.title: Iran Security Chief Killed in Israel Airstrike
news.summary: Ali Larijani, Iran's security chief and one of its most senior leaders, was killed in an Israeli airstrike, confirmed by multiple officials.

news.title: Claude Opus 4.6: OpenAI's Smartest Model
news.summary: OpenAI announced the upgrade to Claude Opus 4.6, described as "our smartest model." The new model delivers state-of-the-art performance across agentic coding, computer use, tool use, search, and finance, often by wide margin. This represents OpenAI's commitment to advancing frontier AI capabilities for practical applications.

news.title: CARE (Covariance-Aware, Rank-Enhanced Decomposition) for Enabling Multi-Head Latent Attention
news.summary: Proposes CARE pipeline for converting GQA (grouped-query attention) to MLA (multi-head latent attention). Addresses low-rank approximation issues: (i) activation-preserving factorization aligning with actual input activations, (ii) adjusted-rank allocation spreading fixed KV budget across layers, (iii) KV-parity reparameterization maintaining MLA format. Outperforms uniform-rank SVD baseline on Qwen3-4B and Llama-3.1-8B, reducing one-shot perplexity by up to 215x and improving mean accuracy by up to 1.70x. ICLR 2026 conference paper.

news.title: Gender Disambiguation in Machine Translation: Diagnostic Evaluation in Decoder-Only Architectures
news.summary: Extends bias evaluation frameworks by introducing "Prior Bias" measure capturing model's default gender assumptions. Applied to decoder-only MT models, reveals that despite scale and SOTA status, these architectures don't generally outperform encoder-decoder on gender-specific metrics. Shows post-training (instruction tuning) improves contextual awareness but reduces masculine Prior Bias rather than eliminating systematic bias.

news.title: MUD (MomentUm Decorrelation) for Faster Transformer Training
news.summary: MUD replaces Muon's polar momentum update with a triangular Cholesky-like whitening surrogate. Shows row-orthonormal matrices are fixed points of MUD map, proving quadratic local convergence near fixed point. Achieves consistent 10-50% wall-clock improvements over AdamW and Muon, with substantially lower optimizer overhead.

news.title: AI-Generated Val Kilmer to Star in New Movie Using Deep As Technology
news.summary: Val Kilmer, who died last December, will appear as a digital facsimile in writer/director Coerte Voorhees' upcoming film As Deep As The Grave. Variety reports that Kilmer's estate has agreed to let an AI-generated facial replica appear in the movie, using technology that was originally developed to create Val Kilmer's likeness in Star Wars. This represents another milestone in using AI for posthumous digital resurrection of performers.

news.title: Lawyers Face $15k Each After Citing Fake Cases in Court Filings
news.summary: Court documents show that lawyers who cited fake cases in filings have been ordered to pay $15,000 each. The filings claimed court orders were "void on its face," but the court ruled otherwise. The substantial penalties highlight serious consequences for attorneys who submit false legal precedents, with additional costs and fees expected to accumulate significantly.

news.title: Meta's NYC Store Becomes Permanent Flagship Location
news.summary: Meta announced that its skateboarding-themed glasses store on Fifth Avenue in Manhattan is no longer a pop-up shop but has become a "permanent flagship location." The store will continue selling Meta's AI smart glasses and Meta Quest headsets, marking a significant expansion of Meta's physical retail presence in major consumer markets.

news.title: Molbook Updates Terms of Service After AI Agent Network Acquisition
news.summary: Days after Meta acquired a "social" network for AI agents, Molbook updated its terms of service, stating that users must be 13 years of age or older to use the site. Users are "solely responsible" for actions taken by their AI agents, "whether they act autonomously or otherwise, and irrespective of whether such actions or omissions were intended." The update reflects growing concerns about accountability for autonomous AI systems.

news.title: NVIDIA CEO Responds to DLSS 5 Backlash: "They're Completely Wrong"
news.summary: NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang responded to gamer criticism of DLSS 5, stating critics are "completely wrong." Huang explained that DLSS 5 fuses controllability of geometry and textures with generative AI, allowing developers to fine-tune generative AI for games. The response addresses concerns about AI integration in game rendering.

news.title: NVIDIA Launches NemoClaw: Secure OpenClaw Platform with Privacy Guardrails
news.summary: NVIDIA announced NemoClaw, an agentic AI platform that adds privacy and security protections to OpenClaw by running it in an "isolated sandbox" environment. NemoClaw uses Nvidia Agent Toolkit software to optimize OpenClaw, providing the missing infrastructure layer for agents to access necessary resources while enforcing policy-based security, network, and privacy guardrails for autonomous operations.

news.title: NVIDIA Planning AI Data Centers in Space with Vera Ruben Space 1
news.summary: NVIDIA is working with partners on a new computer called Vera Ruben Space 1 that will go to space to start data centers out in space. The company acknowledges challenges of cooling systems in space where there's no conduction or convection, only radiation, but states they have "lots of great engineers working on it." This represents a bold expansion into orbital AI infrastructure.

news.title: Sony Training "Protective AI" to Guard Studio Ghibli Content
news.summary: Sony's R&D division is training its own "Protective AI" model on content from Studio Ghibli films, which are popular fodder for generated imitation. Sony hasn't decided what it'll do with the model yet, but it's designed to eventually stop AI from ripping off any protected content. The move represents growing industry action to protect intellectual property from AI infringement in the creative arts sector.

news.title: Trump Comments: "AI Can Be Very Dangerous, We Have to Be Very Careful with It"
news.summary: Former President Trump said "AI can be very dangerous, we have to be very careful with it," emphasizing requirements for AI systems including the ability to "recognize, express, and shift between emotions in a way that feels authentic and human." The comments highlight ongoing debates about AI safety and the need for careful deployment of increasingly powerful AI systems.

news.title: Anticipatory Planning for Multimodal AI Agents (TraceR1)
news.summary: Most existing multimodal agents remain reactive, optimizing actions in isolation without reasoning about future states or long-term goals, limiting planning coherence. TraceR1 introduces a two-stage reinforcement learning framework that explicitly trains anticipatory reasoning by forecasting short-horizon trajectories before execution. First stage performs trajectory-level reinforcement learning with rewards enforcing global consistency across predicted action sequences. Second stage applies grounded reinforcement fine-tuning, using execution feedback from frozen tool agents to refine step-level accuracy and executability.

news.title: CraniMem: Cranial-Inspired Gated and Bounded Memory for Agentic Systems
news.summary: Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly deployed in long-running workflows where they must preserve user and task state across many turns. CraniMem introduces a cranial-inspired gated and bounded memory framework designed to address limitations of existing approaches. The system combines principles of brain memory hierarchies with modern gating mechanisms to create efficient, bounded, and biologically plausible memory architectures for agentic systems.

news.title: Neural-Symbolic Logic Query Answering in Non-Euclidean Space (HYQNET)
news.summary: Answering complex first-order logic (FOL) queries on knowledge graphs is essential for reasoning. HYQNET is a neural-symbolic model for logic query reasoning that fully leverages hyperbolic space, decomposing FOL queries into relation projections and logical operations over fuzzy sets.
